deep_analysis.executive_intro:
This FTSE 100 screen is broadly neutral: 72 names are on hold, 16 are flagged avoid, and only seven earn outright buy or strong-buy ratings (three strong buys, four buys). The actionable set is narrow and skewed toward cyclical, commodity-linked businesses—two of three strong buys sit in Basic Materials (Rio Tinto, Endeavour Mining), with Shell as the lone Energy strong buy—while the two buy-rated names diversify into Consumer Cyclical (Howden Joinery) and Healthcare (Smith & Nephew). Conviction scores across top picks are modest (32%–45%) and every signal is new (one week at signal), so these are fresh quantitative flags rather than established trends. A key caution: data quality is high (19/20 metrics) but several models report missing operating cash flow, NCAV, or skipped earnings-quality checks, and dividend yields in model reasons appear scaled incorrectly (e.g. 293%–445% where summaries show 2.5%–4.5%)—treat yield-dependent model passes with care until manually verified.

**Rio Tinto Group (RIO.L)** — Strong Buy with 10/22 model passes, composite score 74%, sector-relative 77%, and all five factor families clearing (cheapness, quality, dividend, GARP, risk). Quantitative support comes from Graham Enterprising, Neff PEGY, Quality Value, Buffett Quality, High Dividend Yield, Dividend Growth, Magic Formula, Acquirer's Multiple, Composite Value, and Financial Health; key metrics are P/E 14.6, P/B 2.4, yield 4.5%, ROE 16.4%, and D/E 35%. Notable failures include negative earnings growth (growth -5.6%), weak Piotroski F-Score (3/9), FCF yield below the 5% hurdle (3.2%), and earnings quality flagged for weak free-cash conversion. Sector concentration risk is material: Rio is one of two Basic Materials strong buys alongside Endeavour. **Verdict: watchlist** — strong multi-model cheapness and balance-sheet screens pass, but cyclical commodity exposure, declining earnings growth, and low conviction (45%, new signal) argue for staged entry rather than full accumulation.

**Endeavour Mining plc (EDV.L)** — Strong Buy with the highest model count among top picks (11/22), composite 74%, sector-relative 67%, and five families passed. Standout passes include FCF Yield (17.9%), Lynch PEG (PEG 0.13, growth 105.7%), Neff PEGY, Quality Value, Buffett Quality, Economic Moat, Dividend Growth, Magic Formula, Acquirer's Multiple, Composite Value, and Financial Health; metrics read P/E 13.6, P/B 3.5, yield 2.9%, ROE 31.1%, D/E 20%. P/B at 3.5 fails Graham Enterprising and Schloss; Piotroski F-Score is 6/9; earnings quality lacks OCF data. Pairs with Rio in Basic Materials, doubling mining/gold cyclicality in a concentrated top tier. **Verdict: accumulate** — exceptional FCF yield, growth-adjusted valuation (PEG 0.13), and quality screens justify priority sizing, with awareness of commodity price and jurisdiction risk.

**Shell plc (SHEL.L)** — Strong Buy on 9/22 models, composite 62%, sector-relative 81% (best among strong buys vs peers), with cheapness, dividend, GARP, and risk families passed. Passes include Graham Enterprising, FCF Yield (9.1%), Lynch PEG (PEG 0.48, growth 26.6%), Neff PEGY, Dividend Growth, Magic Formula, Acquirer's Multiple, Dreman Contrarian, and Financial Health; P/E 12.8, P/B 1.4, yield 3.8%, ROE 10.7%, D/E 43%. Quality screens fail: Buffett Quality, Quality Value, and Economic Moat all miss on low ROE (10.7%) and thin margins (7.0%); Piotroski F-Score is 3/9. Adds Energy cyclicality alongside the materials cluster. **Verdict: watchlist** — deep value on P/E, P/B, and FCF yield with sector-leading relative score, but sub-12% ROE and weak quality/moat scores warrant confirmation that cash returns are sustainable through the cycle.

**Howden Joinery Group Plc (HWDN.L)** — Buy on 7/22 models, composite 62%, sector-relative 63%, with all five families passed. Passes: FCF Yield (5.0%), Neff PEGY (PEGY 0.06), Quality Value, Buffett Quality, Dividend Growth, Magic Formula, Financial Health; P/E 16.2, P/B 3.6, yield 2.8%, ROE 23.1%, D/E 59%. Lynch PEG fails (PEG 1.76), Economic Moat fails on margins and leverage, Composite Value and Acquirer's Multiple miss, and Piotroski F-Score is 5/9. Reduces sector concentration versus the materials/energy trio. **Verdict: watchlist** — solid UK consumer-cyclical quality metrics and financial health, but housing-cycle sensitivity and failed GARP/moat screens keep it below accumulate until earnings durability is confirmed.

**Smith & Nephew plc (SN.L)** — Buy on 7/22 models, composite 71% (highest among buy-rated names), sector-relative 63%, with cheapness, dividend, GARP, and risk families passed. Passes: Graham Enterprising, FCF Yield (9.0%), Lynch PEG (PEG 0.30, growth 70.4%), Neff PEGY, Dividend Growth, Composite Value, Financial Health; P/E 21.4, P/B 2.5, yield 2.5%, ROE 11.8%, D/E 63%. Quality Value, Buffett Quality, Economic Moat, Magic Formula, and Acquirer's Multiple all fail; Piotroski F-Score is 5/9. Healthcare exposure offers diversification from the cyclical top tier. **Verdict: watchlist** — attractive FCF yield and growth-adjusted valuation, but ROE below quality hurdles, elevated leverage vs Buffett criteria, and below-median dividend rank limit conviction (32%, new signal).

The screen does not capture several qualitative risks that matter for these names:

- **Rio Tinto (RIO.L):** Commodity price cyclicality (iron ore, copper), China demand exposure, ESG and regulatory scrutiny on mining operations, and geopolitical risk in operating jurisdictions. Negative earnings growth (-5.6%) and Piotroski F-Score 3/9 suggest deteriorating fundamental momentum not fully reflected in headline valuation passes. Governance and capital-allocation history (e.g. past impairments, project overruns) are not screened.
- **Endeavour Mining (EDV.L):** Gold-miner cyclicality, West African operational and political risk, and potential M&A/integration risk. P/B 3.5 exceeds deep-value thresholds; earnings growth of 105.7% may be non-recurring and distort PEG metrics. Pension and off-balance-sheet liabilities are not in the dataset.
- **Shell (SHEL.L):** Energy transition and stranded-asset risk, carbon regulation, and oil/gas price volatility. ROE 10.7% and margins 7.0% failed quality screens—returns may compress in a lower-price environment. Legacy litigation and decommissioning liabilities are not captured; governance around capital return vs reinvestment is a judgment call.
- **Howden Joinery (HWDN.L):** UK housing and renovation cyclicality; consumer discretionary downturn risk. D/E 59% and failed Economic Moat (leverage, margins) raise balance-sheet and competitive-moat questions. Pension deficit data is insufficient in the JSON.
- **Smith & Nephew (SN.L):** Medtech competition, product-liability and regulatory approval risk, and hospital-capital-spending cyclicality. D/E 63% failed Buffett Quality; ROE 11.8% is below moat thresholds. Restructuring and margin-recovery execution risk is not quantified in the screen.

**Data limitations:** Earnings Quality failed or was skipped for all five candidates (missing OCF); Graham Net-Net failed on missing NCAV for all; research_verdict fields are null across the board—no qualitative overlay was applied.

**Names worth deeper research:** Endeavour Mining (EDV.L) — strongest growth-adjusted and FCF metrics among strong buys; Rio Tinto (RIO.L) — highest sector-relative score and dividend yield in Basic Materials; Shell (SHEL.L) — deepest absolute valuation (P/B 1.4, P/E 12.8) with sector-leading relative composite, pending quality-of-earnings verification.
